# Gemini Review Pass 1: Fact Checker (RAG-Grounded)

> **You are a fact-checking reviewer for Ukrainian language curriculum.**
> Your ONLY task: compare the module content against authoritative reference sources.
> You do NOT evaluate style, pedagogy, or naturalness — that is a separate reviewer's job.

**IMPORTANT: This content was generated by another AI agent. You are an independent fact-checker.
Compare claims against the provided reference sources. Do NOT evaluate from your own knowledge.**

---

## Module Content

```markdown
## Вступ: Тріада гостинності (Introduction: The Triad of Hospitality)

You learned your first family of Ukrainian verbs — First Conjugation verbs like **читати** (to read) and **знати** (to know), with their characteristic **-аю, -аєш, -ає** endings. You've already taken a huge step: you can talk about actions in the present tense.

Now you're meeting the second great family — **Second Conjugation**, the **-ити** verbs. These verbs have a different set of endings, and together with First Conjugation, they cover nearly every verb you'll need.

### The Hospitality Triad

In Ukrainian culture, there are three things a good host always offers: food, drink, and conversation. When a guest arrives, you feed them, pour them something warm, and sit down to talk. Three verbs capture this perfectly:

- **їсти** — to eat
- **пити** — to drink
- **говорити** — to speak

These three verbs will be your companions throughout this module. Of them, **говорити** is your model Second Conjugation verb. The other two hold surprises — **їсти** is completely irregular, and **пити** actually follows First Conjugation patterns despite its **-ити** ending. You'll learn to handle all three.

> [!culture] **Тріада гостинності**
> In Ukraine, offering food and drink is how you show care. Remember to sit down and eat when invited — it's an expression of welcome. Refusing a meal can feel like rejecting your host's warmth.

### Quick Concept Check

Remember the difference between **doing** something and **completing** something? All the verbs in this module describe **ongoing or habitual actions** — things you do regularly or are doing right now. This is the imperfective aspect you already know from Module 15.

- **Я читаю.** — I'm reading. (ongoing)
- **Я знаю українську мову.** — I know the Ukrainian language. (habitual)

You're building on a solid foundation. Let's go!

## Презентація: Моделі та мутації (Presentation: Paradigms and Mutations)

### The Second Conjugation Pattern

Second Conjugation verbs end in **-ити** in the infinitive. Their present tense endings are built around the vowel **и** (and **-ять** in the **вони** form). Compare with First Conjugation side by side:

| Person | First Conj. (**читати**) | Second Conj. (**говорити**) |
|--------|------------------------|-----------------------------|
| я | читаю | говорю |
| ти | читаєш | говориш |
| він/вона | читає | говорить |
| ми | читаємо | говоримо |
| ви | читаєте | говорите |
| вони | читають | говорять |

The pattern is clear: where First Conjugation uses **є**, Second Conjugation uses **и** (or **і**). The **я** form ends in **-ю** for both, but look at the **вони** form: **-ють** vs **-ять**.

Here are more Second Conjugation verbs in action:

- **Я бачу гарний парк.** — I see a beautiful park.
- **Ти робиш домашнє завдання.** — You're doing homework.
- **Вона любить природу.** — She loves nature.
- **Ми вчимо мову.** — We're learning a language.
- **Вони ходять до школи.** — They walk to school.

### Consonant Mutations: The «Я» Surprise

Some Second Conjugation verbs change their final consonant in the **я** form. This is called **consonant mutation**. It happens because of how sounds evolved in Ukrainian over centuries. Here are the key patterns:

**д → дж:**
- **ходити** → я **ходжу** (NOT ~~я ходю~~)
- **сидіти** → я **сиджу** (NOT ~~я сидю~~)

**с → ш:**
- **просити** → я **прошу** (NOT ~~я просю~~)

**т → ч:**
- **платити** → я **плачу** (NOT ~~я платю~~)

<!-- adapted from: Glazova, Grade 10, §26 — consonant alternation patterns -->

> [!tip] **Only the «я» form mutates!**
> These mutations only affect the **я** form. All other forms keep the original consonant: **ти ходиш**, **він ходить**, **ми ходимо** — no mutation. Just watch out when you say **«я...»**!

### The Labial «Л»

When the verb stem ends in a **labial consonant** — a sound you make with your lips (**б, п, в, м, ф**) — Ukrainian inserts an extra **л** before **-ю** in the **я** form. Think of it as your tongue helping your lips transition to the **ю** sound:

- **робити** → я **роблю** (б + л + ю)
- **любити** → я **люблю** (б + л + ю)

This **л** appears ONLY in the **я** form. Compare: ти **робиш**, він **робить** — no **л** anywhere.

<!-- adapted from: Karaman, Grade 10 — labial consonant + j alternation -->

### The Irregular Verb «Їсти»

The verb **їсти** (to eat) doesn't follow either conjugation pattern. You need to memorize it:

| Person | їсти |
|--------|------|
| я | **їм** |
| ти | **їси** |
| він/вона | **їсть** |
| ми | **їмо** |
| ви | **їсте** |
| вони | **їдять** |

> [!warning] **Common Mistake**
> Never say ~~«я їджу»~~ or ~~«я їстю»~~. The correct form is **«я їм»** — short and simple. Memorize it!

### «Бачити» vs «Дивитися»

English has "see" and "watch" — Ukrainian makes a similar distinction:

- **бачити** — to see (result or ability): **Я бачу сонце.** — I see the sun.
- **дивитися** — to watch (deliberate action): **Я дивлюся.** — I'm watching.

Use **бачити** when something enters your vision naturally. Use **дивитися** when you're actively looking at something. You'll learn to conjugate **дивитися** fully in the next module on reflexive verbs — for now, just know the difference exists.

### A Note on «Пити»

The verb **пити** (to drink) looks like a Second Conjugation verb because it ends in **-ити**. But surprise — it conjugates with First Conjugation endings:

- я **п'ю**, ти **п'єш**, він **п'є**, ми **п'ємо**, ви **п'єте**, вони **п'ють**

Don't let the infinitive fool you! The key to identifying conjugation is the **personal endings**, not the infinitive form.

## Практика: Помилки та автоматизація (Practice: Errors and Automation)

### Don't Mix Your Conjugations!

The most common mistake learners make is putting First Conjugation endings on Second Conjugation verbs. Look at these pairs — only one form is correct:

- ❌ ти роб**еш** → ✅ ти **робиш**
- ❌ він люб**е** → ✅ він **любить**
- ❌ вони ход**ють** → ✅ вони **ходять**

The trick: if the infinitive ends in **-ити** and the **ти** form has **-иш**, you know it's Second Conjugation. When you see **-єш**, that's First Conjugation territory.

### Mutation Drill: The «Я» Form

Practice saying these out loud. Every one has a consonant change in the **я** form:

- ходити → я **ходжу** (д → дж)
- сидіти → я **сиджу** (д → дж)
- просити → я **прошу** (с → ш)
- платити → я **плачу** (т → ч)
- робити → я **роблю** (labial + л)
- любити → я **люблю** (labial + л)

> [!practice] **Say It Out Loud**
> Cover the right column. Look at each infinitive and say the **я** form from memory. Then check yourself. Repeat until you get all six without hesitation!

### Sorting Challenge

Can you sort these verbs into First and Second Conjugation? Look at how they conjugate, not just the infinitive:

**Second Conjugation** (endings: -иш, -ить, -имо, -ите, -ять):
- говорити, робити, бачити, любити, ходити, просити, сидіти, вчити, платити, стояти

**First Conjugation** (endings: -єш, -є, -ємо, -єте, -ють):
- читати, знати, пити (!)

Remember: **пити** ends in **-ити** but conjugates like First Conjugation. The endings always tell the truth!

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Що ти тут робиш?
> — Я сиджу й п'ю каву.
> — А він готує обід?
> — Він їсть борщ.
> — Добре, я теж хочу їсти!
> — Please, sit down! Тут смачно.

## Продукування та культурний контекст (Production and Cultural Context)

### Deep Culture - The Etymology of «Любити»

Here's something beautiful about Ukrainian. The verb **любити** (to love) shares an ancient root with **люди** (people) and **людство** (humanity). In the Proto-Indo-European language family, the root *\*lewdh-* meant "to care for, to be drawn toward." In Ukrainian, love and people grew from the same word. To love is, quite literally, to belong to your community.

- **Я люблю маму.** — I love mom.
- **Люди люблять правду.** — People love truth.

### The Social Weight of Food

In Ukrainian hospitality, **їсти** and **пити** carry deep social meaning. When someone invites you home and says **"Please, sit down and eat!"** — this is more than a meal. It's an act of trust and generosity. Politely refusing food (**«Ні, дякую, я не їм»**) can feel like rejecting your host's goodwill. Even if you're not hungry, it's customary to accept at least a cup of **чай** or **кава**.

> **(Вдома / At home)**
>
> — Привіт! Please, sit down.
> — Дякую! Ти готуєш борщ?
> — Так, я люблю борщ!
> — Добре. А ти п'єш чай? Каву?
> — Я п'ю чай, дякую.

# Підсумок

You've made excellent progress! You can **conjugate Second Conjugation verbs** like **говорити**, **робити**, **бачити**, and **любити** — recognizing their characteristic **-иш, -ить, -имо, -ите, -ять** endings. You can handle **consonant mutations** in the **я** form (**ходжу**, **сиджу**, **прошу**, **плачу**) and the **labial л** pattern (**роблю**, **люблю**). You know that **їсти** is irregular (**я їм!**) and that **пити** secretly follows First Conjugation despite its **-ити** ending.

Most importantly, you can now talk about the core activities of Ukrainian social life — eating, drinking, and speaking together.

### Self-Check

Ask yourself:

1. What are the present tense endings for Second Conjugation verbs?
2. What happens to **д** in the **я** form of **ходити**?
3. Why does **робити** become **роблю** (not ~~робю~~)?
4. What is the correct **я** form of **їсти**?

If you can answer all four, you're ready for the next module — **Reflexive Verbs**, where you'll meet the particle **-ся/-сь** and learn verbs like **дивитися** (to watch) and **вчитися** (to study). Great work — you're doing beautifully!

```

```yaml
module: a1-016
level: A1
sequence: 16
slug: the-living-verb-ii
version: '2.0'
title: The Living Verb II
subtitle: Second Conjugation (-ити verbs)
focus: grammar
pedagogy: PPP
phase: A1.2 [Verbs & Sentences]
word_target: 1200
objectives:
- Learner can conjugate Second Conjugation verbs (-ити) in present tense
- Learner can distinguish between First and Second Conjugation
- Learner can use common irregular verbs (їсти, пити)
- Learner can form basic sentences with action verbs
content_outline:
- section: 'Вступ: Тріада гостинності (Introduction: The Triad of Hospitality)'
  words: 250
  points:
  - 'Introduction to Second Conjugation verbs (-ити): establishing this family as the second pillar of Ukrainian verbal action,
    focusing on ongoing and habitual meanings.'
  - 'Cultural Motivator - The Triad of Hospitality: Establishing the verbs ''їсти'' (to eat), ''пити'' (to drink), and ''говорити''
    (to speak) as the core of Ukrainian social interaction, where connection is built through feeding and conversation.'
  - 'Concept check: reviewing the difference between ''doing'' (imperfective) and ''completing'', anchoring the module in
    the learner''s existing understanding of present tense from Module 06.'
- section: 'Презентація: Моделі та мутації (Presentation: Paradigms and Mutations)'
  words: 375
  points:
  - 'Systematic introduction of endings: -ити → -ю, -иш, -ить, -имо, -ите, -ять. Side-by-side comparison with First Conjugation
    (читати vs говорити) to highlight the critical vowel difference (е/є vs и/і).'
  - 'State Standard competency (§4.2.4.1): Using ''сидіти'' (я сиджу) as the primary model for consonant mutation (д→дж) in
    the first person singular.'
  - 'Phonetic framing of the ''Labial L'' in ''люблю'' and ''роблю'': teaching the epenthetic ''l'' not just as a rule, but
    as a physical aid to separate labial consonants (б, п, в, м, ф) from the ''ю'' sound.'
  - 'The irregularity of ''їсти'' (to eat): explicitly teaching the correct ''я їм'' form and contrasting it with the common
    learner error ''я їджу'' or ''я їстю''.'
  - Distinction between 'бачити' (to see - result/faculty) and 'дивитися' (to watch - process) to prevent semantic confusion
    in early sentence building.
- section: 'Практика: Помилки та автоматизація (Practice: Errors and Automation)'
  words: 300
  points:
  - 'Contrastive drills to prevent Conjugation Mixing: identifying and correcting the habit of applying 1st conjugation endings
    to 2nd conjugation verbs (e.g., ''ти робиш'' vs incorrect ''ти робеш'').'
  - 'Mutation Mastery: intensive ''Я''-form drills with verbs like ''ходити'' (ходжу), ''сидіти'' (сиджу), and ''платити''
    (плачу) to eliminate omission errors like ''я ходю'' or ''я сидю''.'
  - 'Sorting exercises: categorization of 1st and 2nd conjugation verbs to build structural intuition for vowel patterns in
    endings.'
- section: Продукування та культурний контекст (Production and Cultural Context)
  words: 275
  points:
  - 'Contextual Production: describing daily routines and interests using high-frequency collocations such as ''робити домашнє
    завдання'', ''любити природу'', and ''говорити українською''.'
  - 'Deep Culture - The Etymology of ''Любити'': exploring the PIE root (*lewdh-) shared with ''люди'' (people) and ''людство''
    (humanity), illustrating how love is intrinsically linked to community belonging.'
  - 'Social Ethics of Hospitality: discussing the weight of ''їсти'' and ''пити'' in hosting, noting that refusing a meal
    (''не їсти'') can be perceived as a rejection of the host''s goodwill.'
vocabulary_hints:
  required:
  - говорити (to speak) — говорити українською, говорити правду; High Frequency (Top 100). Part of the 'Hospitality Triad'.
  - 'робити (to do) — робити домашнє завдання, робити покупки, робити вибір; High Frequency (Top 50). Note labial mutation:
    ''я роблю''.'
  - бачити (to see) — я бачу, радий бачити, бачити на власні очі; High Frequency (Top 200). Focus on result/faculty.
  - 'любити (to love) — я люблю тебе, любити природу, любити читати; shares PIE root with ''люди'' (people). Note labial mutation:
    ''я люблю''.'
  - 'їсти (to eat) — хотіти їсти, смачно їсти; Irregular: ''я їм'', ''ти їси'', ''він їсть''. Learner error: avoid ''я їджу''.'
  - пити (to drink) — пити каву, пити воду; High Frequency. Essential for cultural hospitality contexts.
  - ходити (to walk) — ходити в парк, ходити до школи; involves mutation 'я ходжу' (д→дж).
  - просити (to ask) — просити допомоги; involves mutation 'я прошу' (с→ш).
  recommended:
  - сидіти (to sit) — State Standard example for consonant mutation (я сиджу).
  - стояти (to stand) — high frequency positional verb; note 'вони стоять' ending.
  - платити (to pay) — useful for practical scenarios; involves mutation 'я плачу' (т→ч).
  - вчити (to teach/learn) — вчити мову; frequent 2nd conjugation verb with 'и' stem.
activity_hints:

  items: 30
- type: match-up

- Consonant mutation patterns

register: розмовний
```

## Activities YAML

```yaml
- type: fill-in
  title: "Conjugate Second Conjugation Verbs"
  instruction: "Fill in the correct present tense form of the Second Conjugation verb."
  items:
    - sentence: "Я ___ українською мовою. (говорити)"
      answer: "говорю"
      options: ["говорю", "говоряю", "говоріть", "говорять"]
      explanation: "Говорити is Second Conjugation: я говорю."
    - sentence: "Ти ___ домашнє завдання. (робити)"
      answer: "робиш"
      options: ["робиш", "робеш", "робіть", "роблю"]
      explanation: "Second Conjugation ти-form uses -иш: ти робиш."
    - sentence: "Він ___ гарний парк. (бачити)"
      answer: "бачить"
      options: ["бачить", "бачить", "бачає", "бачу"]
      explanation: "Second Conjugation він-form uses -ить: він бачить."
    - sentence: "Ми ___ мову. (вчити)"
      answer: "вчимо"
      options: ["вчимо", "вчаємо", "вчемо", "вчить"]
      explanation: "Second Conjugation ми-form uses -имо: ми вчимо."
    - sentence: "Ви ___ правду. (говорити)"
      answer: "говорите"
      options: ["говорите", "говоряєте", "говоріть", "говорять"]
      explanation: "Second Conjugation ви-form uses -ите: ви говорите."
    - sentence: "Вони ___ до школи. (ходити)"
      answer: "ходять"
      options: ["ходять", "ходють", "ходимо", "ходжу"]
      explanation: "Second Conjugation вони-form uses -ять: вони ходять."
    - sentence: "Вона ___ природу. (любити)"
      answer: "любить"
      options: ["любить", "любає", "люблю", "любимо"]
      explanation: "Second Conjugation він/вона-form uses -ить: вона любить."
    - sentence: "Ми ___ допомоги. (просити)"
      answer: "просимо"
      options: ["просимо", "просаємо", "прошу", "просять"]
      explanation: "Second Conjugation ми-form uses -имо: ми просимо."

- type: fill-in
  title: "The Tricky Я-Form"
  instruction: "Fill in the correct я-form. Watch for consonant mutations and the labial л!"
  items:
    - sentence: "Я ___ до школи. (ходити)"
      answer: "ходжу"
      options: ["ходжу", "ходю", "ходю", "ходить"]
      explanation: "Mutation д → дж in the я-form: я ходжу."
    - sentence: "Я ___ на стільці. (сидіти)"
      answer: "сиджу"
      options: ["сиджу", "сидю", "сидіть", "сидять"]
      explanation: "Mutation д → дж: я сиджу (not сидю)."
    - sentence: "Я ___ допомоги. (просити)"
      answer: "прошу"
      options: ["прошу", "просю", "просить", "просимо"]
      explanation: "Mutation с → ш: я прошу (not просю)."
    - sentence: "Я ___ за каву. (платити)"
      answer: "плачу"
      options: ["плачу", "платю", "платить", "платимо"]
      explanation: "Mutation т → ч: я плачу (not платю)."
    - sentence: "Я ___ домашнє завдання. (робити)"
      answer: "роблю"
      options: ["роблю", "робю", "робиш", "робить"]
      explanation: "Labial б + л: я роблю (not робю)."
    - sentence: "Я ___ маму. (любити)"
      answer: "люблю"
      options: ["люблю", "любю", "любиш", "любить"]
      explanation: "Labial б + л: я люблю (not любю)."

- type: fill-in
  title: "Irregular Verbs: Їсти and Пити"
  instruction: "Fill in the correct form of їсти or пити."
  items:
    - sentence: "Я ___ борщ. (їсти)"
      answer: "їм"
      options: ["їм", "їджу", "їстю", "їсти"]
      explanation: "Їсти is irregular: я їм (not їджу or їстю)."
    - sentence: "Ти ___ каву. (пити)"
      answer: "п'єш"
      options: ["п'єш", "пиш", "пиєш", "п'ю"]
      explanation: "Пити follows First Conjugation endings: ти п'єш."
    - sentence: "Він ___ обід. (їсти)"
      answer: "їсть"
      options: ["їсть", "їдить", "їстить", "їм"]
      explanation: "Irregular: він їсть."
    - sentence: "Ми ___ чай. (пити)"
      answer: "п'ємо"
      options: ["п'ємо", "пимо", "п'єте", "п'ють"]
      explanation: "Пити with First Conjugation endings: ми п'ємо."
    - sentence: "Вони ___ борщ. (їсти)"
      answer: "їдять"
      options: ["їдять", "їстять", "їмо", "їсть"]
      explanation: "Irregular: вони їдять."
    - sentence: "Я ___ воду. (пити)"
      answer: "п'ю"
      options: ["п'ю", "пию", "пиєш", "п'ємо"]
      explanation: "Пити follows First Conjugation: я п'ю."

- type: match-up
  title: "Match Infinitive to Я-Form"
  instruction: "Match each verb infinitive to its correct я-form."
  pairs:
    - left: "говорити"
      right: "говорю"
    - left: "ходити"
      right: "ходжу"
    - left: "сидіти"
      right: "сиджу"
    - left: "просити"
      right: "прошу"
    - left: "платити"
      right: "плачу"
    - left: "робити"
      right: "роблю"
    - left: "любити"
      right: "люблю"
    - left: "їсти"
      right: "їм"

- type: match-up
  title: "Match Verb to Translation"
  instruction: "Match each Ukrainian verb to its English meaning."
  pairs:
    - left: "говорити"
      right: "to speak"
    - left: "робити"
      right: "to do / to make"
    - left: "бачити"
      right: "to see"
    - left: "любити"
      right: "to love"
    - left: "їсти"
      right: "to eat"
    - left: "пити"
      right: "to drink"
    - left: "ходити"
      right: "to walk / to go"
    - left: "просити"
      right: "to ask / to request"

- type: group-sort
  title: "First or Second Conjugation?"
  instruction: "Sort these verbs into the correct conjugation group based on their endings."
  groups:
    - name: "First Conjugation (-єш, -є, -ємо)"
      items:
        - "читати"
        - "знати"
        - "пити"
    - name: "Second Conjugation (-иш, -ить, -имо)"
      items:
        - "говорити"
        - "робити"
        - "бачити"
        - "любити"
        - "ходити"
        - "просити"
        - "вчити"

- type: quiz
  title: "Conjugation Knowledge Check"
  instruction: "Choose the correct answer."
  items:
    - question: "What are the characteristic endings of Second Conjugation verbs?"
      options:
        - text: "-иш, -ить, -имо, -ите, -ять"
          correct: true
        - text: "-єш, -є, -ємо, -єте, -ють"
          correct: false
        - text: "-аш, -ать, -амо, -ате, -ають"
          correct: false
        - text: "-іш, -ість, -імо, -іте, -іять"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Second Conjugation uses -иш, -ить, -имо, -ите, -ять. First Conjugation uses -єш, -є, -ємо, -єте, -ють."
    - question: "What happens to д in the я-form of ходити?"
      options:
        - text: "д changes to дж (я ходжу)"
          correct: true
        - text: "д stays the same (я ходю)"
          correct: false
        - text: "д changes to ж (я хожу)"
          correct: false
        - text: "д disappears (я хою)"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Consonant mutation: д → дж. So ходити → я ходжу."
    - question: "Why does робити become роблю in the я-form?"
      options:
        - text: "A л is inserted between the labial б and ю"
          correct: true
        - text: "The б changes to бл"
          correct: false
        - text: "It is completely irregular"
          correct: false
        - text: "The о changes to ол"
          correct: false
      explanation: "When the stem ends in a labial consonant (б, п, в, м, ф), Ukrainian inserts л before ю."
    - question: "What is the correct я-form of їсти?"
      options:
        - text: "я їм"
          correct: true
        - text: "я їджу"
          correct: false
        - text: "я їстю"
          correct: false
        - text: "я їсю"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Їсти is irregular. The correct form is simply я їм."
    - question: "Пити ends in -ити, so which conjugation does it follow?"
      options:
        - text: "First Conjugation (п'єш, п'є, п'ємо)"
          correct: true
        - text: "Second Conjugation (пиш, пить, пимо)"
          correct: false
        - text: "It is completely irregular"
          correct: false
        - text: "It follows both conjugations"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Despite ending in -ити, пити conjugates with First Conjugation endings: п'ю, п'єш, п'є."
    - question: "Which mutation pattern does просити follow in the я-form?"
      options:
        - text: "с → ш (я прошу)"
          correct: true
        - text: "с → з (я прозю)"
          correct: false
        - text: "с → сь (я просью)"
          correct: false
        - text: "No mutation (я просю)"
          correct: false
      explanation: "The mutation is с → ш: просити → я прошу."

- type: true-false
  title: "True or False?"
  instruction: "Decide whether each statement about Ukrainian verbs is true or false."
  items:
    - statement: "Second Conjugation verbs use the ending -ять in the вони-form."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Correct! Вони говорять, ходять, бачать — Second Conjugation uses -ять."
    - statement: "Consonant mutations happen in ALL forms of Second Conjugation verbs."
      correct: false
      explanation: "Mutations only happen in the я-form. Other forms keep the original consonant: ти ходиш, він ходить."
    - statement: "The correct я-form of їсти is я їджу."
      correct: false
      explanation: "Їсти is irregular: the correct form is я їм."
    - statement: "Пити conjugates like a First Conjugation verb despite ending in -ити."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Correct! Пити uses First Conjugation endings: п'ю, п'єш, п'є, п'ємо, п'єте, п'ють."
    - statement: "When a verb stem ends in a labial consonant (б, п, в, м, ф), a л is inserted before ю in the я-form."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Correct! This gives us роблю (not робю) and люблю (not любю)."
    - statement: "The verb бачити means 'to watch' in Ukrainian."
      correct: false
      explanation: "Бачити means 'to see' (result/ability). Дивитися means 'to watch' (deliberate action)."
    - statement: "If the ти-form has the ending -иш, the verb is Second Conjugation."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Correct! The -иш ending is the hallmark of Second Conjugation: ти робиш, ти говориш."
    - statement: "The mutation pattern for платити in the я-form is т → ч."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Correct! Платити → я плачу (т → ч)."

- type: unjumble
  title: "Build Ukrainian Sentences"
  instruction: "Arrange the words to form a correct Ukrainian sentence."
  items:
    - words: ["українською", "Я", "говорю"]
      answer: "Я говорю українською"
    - words: ["бачу", "парк", "Я", "гарний"]
      answer: "Я бачу гарний парк"
    - words: ["робиш", "завдання", "Ти", "домашнє"]
      answer: "Ти робиш домашнє завдання"
    - words: ["природу", "любить", "Вона"]
      answer: "Вона любить природу"
    - words: ["ходять", "школи", "Вони", "до"]
      answer: "Вони ходять до школи"
    - words: ["мову", "вчимо", "Ми"]
      answer: "Ми вчимо мову"

- type: quiz
  title: "Spot the Wrong Form"
  instruction: "Which verb form contains an error?"
  items:
    - question: "Which form is INCORRECT?"
      options:
        - text: "ти робеш"
          correct: true
        - text: "ти робиш"
          correct: false
        - text: "він робить"
          correct: false
        - text: "ми робимо"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Робити is Second Conjugation, so ти-form is робиш (not робеш with First Conjugation ending)."
    - question: "Which form is INCORRECT?"
      options:
        - text: "вони ходють"
          correct: true
        - text: "вони ходять"
          correct: false
        - text: "ти ходиш"
          correct: false
        - text: "він ходить"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Second Conjugation вони-form is -ять: вони ходять (not ходють)."
    - question: "Which form is INCORRECT?"
      options:
        - text: "я ходю"
          correct: true
        - text: "я ходжу"
          correct: false
        - text: "ти ходиш"
          correct: false
        - text: "ми ходимо"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Mutation д → дж in the я-form: я ходжу (not ходю)."
    - question: "Which form is INCORRECT?"
      options:
        - text: "я робю"
          correct: true
        - text: "я роблю"
          correct: false
        - text: "він робить"
          correct: false
        - text: "вони роблять"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Labial б needs л before ю: я роблю (not робю)."
    - question: "Which form is INCORRECT?"
      options:
        - text: "він любе"
          correct: true
        - text: "він любить"
          correct: false
        - text: "я люблю"
          correct: false
        - text: "ти любиш"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Любити is Second Conjugation: він любить (not любе with First Conjugation ending)."
    - question: "Which form is INCORRECT?"
      options:
        - text: "я їджу"
          correct: true
        - text: "я їм"
          correct: false
        - text: "ти їси"
          correct: false
        - text: "він їсть"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Їсти is irregular: я їм (not їджу)."
```

---

## Research Notes

# Дослідження: The Living Verb II

## State Standard Reference
§4.2.4.1: "Indicative mood — present, past, future tenses; conjugation I/II; reflexive verbs (-ся/-сь)"
Alignment: This module delivers the Second Conjugation half of the §4.2.4.1 indicative/conjugation requirement. Conjugation I was introduced in a1-15; together they complete the A1 verb paradigm.

## Vocabulary Frequency

| Word | Notes | Key collocations |
|------|-------|------------------|
| говорити | Top 100; core communication verb | говорити українською, говорити правду |
| робити | Top 50; labial mutation: я роблю | робити домашнє завдання, робити вибір |
| любити | Shares PIE root *lewdh- with люди; labial mutation: я люблю | любити природу, любити читати |
| їсти | Irregular: я їм / ти їси / він їсть; error target | хотіти їсти, добре їсти |
| пити | Regular -ити verb; hospitality context | пити каву, пити воду |
| бачити | Resultative/faculty perception; я бачу (no mutation) | радий бачити, бачити на власні очі |
| ходити | Mutation: д→дж → я ходжу | ходити в парк, ходити до школи |
| просити | Mutation: с→ш → я прошу | просити допомоги |
| сидіти | State Standard mutation model: д→дж → я сиджу | сидіти вдома |
| платити | Mutation: т→ч → я плачу | платити за каву |

## Cultural Hooks

1. **Hospitality Triad**: In Ukrainian culture, hosting guests means offering їжу (food), напої (drinks), and розмову (conversation). Refusing to eat what a host prepares — "я не їм" — can carry an unintended social message of rejection. The verbs їсти, пити, говорити are not just high-frequency words; they are the grammar of belonging.

2. **Etymology of любити**: The root *lewdh- connects любити (to love) → люди (people) → людство (humanity). This is a concrete, memorable etymological hook: to love is literally "to be drawn toward people." Useful as a cultural-linguistic aside in the production section.

3. **Прислів'я about speaking**: "Говори мало, слухай багато, думай ще більше." — common Ukrainian folk saying that introduces говорити in a memorable cultural frame. All words are within A1 scope except більше (comparative), which can be glossed.

## Common Learner Errors

1. **Conjugation mixing** — applying 1st conj. endings to 2nd conj. verbs: *ти робеш* → **ти робиш**. Cause: -ити verbs also have infinitive -ти like 1st conj. verbs; learners over-generalize.
2. **Mutation omission in я-form** — *я ходю* / *я сидю* → **я ходжу** / **я сиджу**. Cause: mutation feels arbitrary without phonetic framing; the д→дж / с→ш / т→ч pattern needs explicit naming.
3. **Irregular їсти** — *я їджу* / *я їстю* → **я їм**. Cause: learners assume regular -ити endings apply; їсти is a suppletive outlier requiring rote memorization.
4. **бачити vs дивитися confusion** — using *я бачу* to mean "I am watching" → **я дивлюся**. Cause: English "see/watch" distinction doesn't map cleanly; bачити = involuntary/faculty, дивитися = deliberate action.

## Cross-References
- Builds on: a1-15 (The Living Verb I — 1st Conjugation, present tense paradigm)
- Prepares for: a1-17 (Reflexive Verbs — дивитися, вчитися will reappear), a1-21 (Past Tense — mutation patterns recur in past tense forms)

## Teaching Strategy
- **Discovery exercise**: Present a short scene without explanation — a table at a Ukrainian kitchen: "Бабуся готує. Онук сидить. Мама говорить. Тато їсть борщ. Дід п'є чай." Ask the learner: what are the endings on all these verbs? What vowel do you see? The learner notices -ить, -ить, -ить, -ть — a pattern emerges before the rule is named. Then contrast with First Conjugation: читає, пише, знає — different vowel. This two-column contrast (е/є vs и/і) is the discovery anchor.
- **Best context**: A Ukrainian kitchen/meal scene (hospitality triad: їсти, пити, говорити) is the most natural and culturally resonant frame. Supports практика section activities.
- **Contrastive pairs**: говорити / читати (2nd vs 1st, same -ти infinitive); бачити / дивитися (see vs watch, state vs process); я люблю / я любиш (mutation vs no-mutation form).

## Notes for Content Writing
- Labial mutation (люблю, роблю) should be introduced as a **phonetic phenomenon** — "your lips need a buffer before ю" — not just a spelling rule.
- їсти paradigm must appear in a dedicated box; do not bury it in running text.
- стояти ending вони стоять is an irregular-looking form worth noting as a pattern (стояти, кричати belong to -а stem group, but their endings are identical to -ити group for most persons).
- Keep mutation inventory manageable: focus on д→дж (ходжу, сиджу), с→ш (прошу), т→ч (плачу), labials +л (люблю, роблю). Do not introduce б→бл, п→пл, в→вл separately — treat as "labials all get +л".


## Resource Discovery

### Videos
- [ULP 3-94 У піцерії – At the pizzeria + Sound changes between imperfective and perfective verbs in...](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zVdb0g7KDC0) (Ukrainian Lessons) — The video explicitly discusses 'доконаний і недоконаний вид дієслів' (perfective and imperfective verbs) and 'чергування зміни' (sound changes/alternations), which directly corresponds to the module's 'Презентація: Моделі та мутації' section on verb paradigms and their transformations.
- [ULP 1-34 | Going to the movies in Ukraine – Perfective verbs in Ukrainian](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0sWUV72QiSU) (Ukrainian Lessons) — This video introduces the concept of 'perfective and imperfective verbs', which is a fundamental grammatical aspect directly related to the 'Paradigms and Mutations' of verbs, central to 'The Living Verb II' module.
- [Ukrainian for beginners: Where are u from? Countries, languages | Genitive case,  Verb conjugations](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xGrdJHym-9E) (Ukrainian with Olha) — The video explicitly states it teaches 'how to conjugate verbs of the first conjugation type'. Verb conjugation is a core concept for understanding 'Paradigms and Mutations' within 'The Living Verb II' module.
- [Ukrainian for beginners: Hobbies | Ukrainian music | Palatalization, Verb + infinitive, Noun cases](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A49aZdwMz6k) (Ukrainian with Olha) — The video discusses 'verb plus infinitive construction'. While not as broad as aspect or conjugation, it covers a specific usage pattern of verbs, which is relevant to 'Practice: Errors and Automation' or 'Продукування та культурний контекст' for verb application.
- [Cooking verbs in Ukrainian. Дієслова для готування.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_lvdp6hRm1U) (Bright Kids Ukrainian) — The video focuses on 'cooking verbs', which is a thematic vocabulary set. While it addresses 'дієслова', its specific focus does not directly align with the grammatical topics (e.g., paradigms, mutations, hospitality triad) of the module 'The Living Verb II'.

## Friction Constraints (Past Review Findings)

FRICTION CONSTRAINTS (from past build reviews — DO NOT repeat these errors):
- [GLOBAL] сес-тра is a VALID word division per Правопис 2019 §49. Do NOT mark it as an error. Phonetic syllabification (се-стра) and typographic word division (сес-тра) follow different rules — both are correct in their respective contexts.
- [GLOBAL] Ukrainian textbooks teach a hands-on-EARS test for voicing (закрий долонями вуха), NOT a hand-on-throat test. The hand-on-throat test is a valid phonetics technique but must NOT be attributed to Ukrainian textbooks. Source: Кравцова 2019, Grade 2, p.39.
- [GLOBAL] Do NOT invent Ukrainian words for minimal pairs. "Сір" is NOT a word meaning "grey" — the correct form is "сірий". Use verified minimal pairs only: кит/кіт, бити/біти, лис/ліс.

- `аєш` (source: prose)
- `бачає` (source: activities)
- `бл` (source: activities)
- `вчаємо` (source: activities)
- `вчемо` (source: activities)
- `говоряю` (source: activities)
- `говоряєте` (source: activities)
- `дж` (source: prose)
- ... and 39 more

**Action:** Check if these are valid Ukrainian word forms. Proper nouns and vocative forms may be legitimate. Hallucinated forms or Russianisms must be flagged.

## Pre-Computed Metrics

```
Word count:       1396 / 1200 (116.3%)
Activities:       10
Vocabulary items: 20
Immersion:        19.1%
```

---

## Your Task: Plan Adherence + Factual Verification

### PART A: Plan Adherence Check (MANDATORY)

The plan YAML above is the source of truth for what the module MUST contain. Check EVERY point systematically:

#### A1. Content Outline Points

For EACH section in `content_outline`, and for EACH `points` entry within that section:
1. Search the module content for evidence that this point is addressed
2. Classify as:
   - **COVERED** — the point is clearly addressed in the content. Quote the relevant sentence(s).
   - **MISSING** — the point does not appear in the content. This is a **MEDIUM** severity issue.
   - **PARTIAL** — some aspect is covered but key details are missing. Note what's missing.

**Output a checklist:**
```
### Plan Point Coverage
- Section "Вступ — Introduction":
  - [x] Point 1: "A1 immersion 10-50%..." — COVERED (line ~5: "...")
  - [ ] Point 2: "Cultural hook: Cyrillic script..." — MISSING
  ...
```

#### A2. Required Vocabulary

For EACH word in `vocabulary_hints.required`:
1. Search the module content (not just the word list section — the entire prose)
2. Check if it appears in the activities YAML (if provided in the content above)
3. Classify as:
   - **PRESENT** — found in prose AND activities
   - **PROSE ONLY** — in prose but not in any activity (LOW)
   - **MISSING** — not in prose at all (**HIGH** severity)

#### A3. Activity Hints

For EACH entry in `activity_hints`:
1. Check if an activity of that `type` exists
2. Check if the `focus` matches
3. Check if the `items` count is met
4. Flag missing activity types as **MEDIUM**

#### A4. Section Word Budgets

For EACH section in `content_outline` with a `words` target:
1. Estimate the word count of the corresponding content section
2. Flag sections >10% under their target as **MEDIUM**

#### A5. Pronunciation Videos (if plan has `pronunciation_videos`)

Check that video links from the plan are embedded in the content. Flag missing embeds as **MEDIUM**.

---

### PART B: Factual Verification

For EVERY grammar explanation, factual claim, cultural assertion, and historical reference in the module content:

1. **Search the reference sources above** for confirming or contradicting evidence
2. **Classify each claim** as one of:
   - **CONFIRMED [Tier 1]** — matches a reference source. Cite the source (chunk ID or source identifier) and quote both the module claim and the reference.
   - **DISCREPANCY [Tier 1]** — contradicts a reference source. HIGH severity. Cite both the module claim and the contradicting reference with chunk ID. Suggest the correct version.
   - **UNVERIFIED** — no reference coverage. Flag explicitly. Do NOT evaluate from your own knowledge. Do NOT mark as correct or incorrect — just "UNVERIFIED".

### Citation Enforcement

Every DISCREPANCY entry MUST include:
- Exact quote from the module content
- Exact quote from the reference source
- **Chunk ID or source identifier** (e.g., "Textbook, Grade 3, chunk_abc123" or "VESUM: lemma X")

Free-text citations with no chunk ID or source identifier carry ZERO weight and will be programmatically discarded.

### Special Checks

1. **Callout boxes** (`[!did-you-know]`, `[!myth-buster]`, `[!culture-note]`, `[!fun-fact]`): These are the #1 source of fabrication. Scrutinize every claim.
2. **Grammar rules**: Verify rule statements against textbook references. Flag overgeneralizations.
3. **Etymologies**: Verify word origins against references. Flag folk etymologies.
4. **Statistics and dates**: Flag unsourced numbers.
5. **VESUM failures**: Words not found in VESUM may be misspellings or Russianisms.

---

## Output Format

You MUST use these exact delimiters:

```
===FACTUAL_REVIEW_START===
## Plan Adherence Summary

**Content outline points:** N total, N covered, N missing, N partial
**Required vocabulary:** N total, N present, N missing
**Activity hints:** N total, N matched, N missing
**Plan Adherence Score:** X/10
(10 = all points covered; deduct 1 per MISSING content point, 2 per MISSING required word)

### Plan Point Coverage

[Checklist from Part A1 — every point with [x] or [ ] and evidence]

### Required Vocabulary Coverage

| Word | In Prose? | In Activities? | Status |
|------|-----------|---------------|--------|
| ... | YES/NO | YES/NO | PRESENT / PROSE ONLY / MISSING |

### Activity Hints Coverage

| Hint Type | Focus | Required Items | Found? | Actual Items |
|-----------|-------|---------------|--------|-------------|
| ... | ... | N | YES/NO | N |

### Missing Plan Points (Fix Targets)

[List each MISSING or PARTIAL point with the exact plan text and which section it belongs to.
These become fix targets in the SECTION_FIX block below.]

---

## Factual Verification Summary

**Total claims checked:** N
**Confirmed [Tier 1]:** N
**Discrepancies [Tier 1]:** N
**Unverified:** N
**Factual Alignment Score:** X/10

## Confirmed Claims

[List confirmed claims with source citations]

## Discrepancies

### Discrepancy 1: [brief title]
- **Module says:** "[exact quote from module]"
- **Reference says:** "[exact quote from reference source]"
- **Source:** [chunk ID / source identifier]
- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Suggested fix:** [what to change]

[... more discrepancies ...]

## Unverified Claims

[List claims with no reference coverage]

## Verdict

**Status:** PASS / FAIL
(FAIL if any HIGH severity discrepancy OR 3+ MISSING plan points found)
===FACTUAL_REVIEW_END===
```

If you find fixable discrepancies, also output FIND/REPLACE pairs:

```
===SECTION_FIX_START===
FILE: /Users/krisztiankoos/projects/learn-ukrainian/curriculum/l2-uk-en/a1/the-living-verb-ii.md
FIND:
[exact text from module with the error]
REPLACE:
[corrected text based on reference source]
---
[... more pairs, max 15 ...]
===SECTION_FIX_END===
```

---

## Boundaries

- Do NOT evaluate style, naturalness, or pedagogy — that is Pass 2's job
- Do NOT score dimensions (except Plan Adherence and Factual Alignment) — that is Pass 2's job
- Do NOT evaluate factual claims from your own knowledge — ONLY compare against provided references
- Plan adherence checking uses the plan YAML as ground truth — this is objective, not subjective
- Do NOT fabricate references — if no reference covers a claim, mark it UNVERIFIED
- Maximum 15 FIND/REPLACE pairs (prioritize MISSING plan points over minor factual fixes)
